qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Denial of Service via unbounded multipart form data on unauthenticated webhook endpoints
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: This finding concerns multipart/form-data request parsing on unauthenticated webhook endpoints, not the Data Table Get node orderByColumn SQL query construction described by the advisory.

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: SSRF in workflow import-from-url endpoint allows authenticated users to probe internal services
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: This finding concerns GET /rest/workflows/from-url fetching an attacker-supplied URL with axios, not SQL injection in the Data Table Get node orderByColumn expression.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No final qualifying finding identifies the advisory-described vulnerable code or root cause: SQL injection in n8n's Data Table Get node through an orderByColumn expression. The manifest-listed qualifying findings cover unrelated webhook multipart parsing and workflow import URL fetching paths.
evidence: Advisory GHSA-98c2-4cr3-4jc3 describes SQL injection in the n8n Data Table Get node via orderByColumn expression, with PostgreSQL multi-statement execution enabling data modification or deletion.
dos-multipart-webhook/vulnerability_report.json identifies unbounded multipart/form-data parsing on webhook endpoints and does not identify Data Table, orderByColumn, or SQL query construction.
ssrf-workflows-from-url/vulnerability_report.json identifies raw axios.get(query.url) in GET /rest/workflows/from-url and does not identify Data Table, orderByColumn, or SQL injection.
A targeted session search for orderByColumn, Data Table, datatable, and SQL-injection terms found no qualifying finding evidence tied to the advisory root cause.
